content.problemStatement:
You have 222 piles of stones. Initially, one pile has 000 stones, and the other has 111 stone with weight WWW.

You have NNN boxes where the ithi^{th}ith box contains infinite number of stones, each with weight AiA_iAi​ such that Ai<Ai+1A_i \lt A_{i + 1}Ai​<Ai+1​ for all 1≤i<N1 \le i \lt N1≤i<N.

One move is defined in the following way:


Choose a box and take exactly one stone from that box;
Add the stone to the **lighter pile** (the pile currently with the lower total weight). You must ensure that this pile now becomes the **heavier pile**.
More formally, let the current weights of the 222 piles be W1W_1W1​ and W2W_2W2​ (W1<W2)(W_1 \lt W_2)(W1​<W2​). Then, you can choose the ithi^{th}ith box if and only if W1+Ai>W2W_1 + A_i \gt W_2W1​+Ai​>W2​.
constraints: The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and WWW — the number of boxes and the initial weight of the second pile respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the weight of each stone in the ithi^{th}ith pile.
For each test case, in the first line, output X (1≤X≤7500)X \ (1 \le X \le 7500)X (1≤X≤7500), the length of the sequence you generated or −1-1−1 if there is no possible sequence.
If there is a possible sequence, on the next line, output XXX integers, the ithi^{th}ith being the index of the box used in the ithi^{th}ith move.
Ai<Ai+1A_i \lt A_{i + 1}Ai​<Ai+1​ for all 1≤i<N1 \le i \lt N1≤i<N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A possible sequence of moves would be:

Add the stone with weight 666 to the first pile, bringing the weights to 666 and 555.
Add the stone with weight 333 to the second pile, bringing the weights to 666 and 888.

We are done since we have used all boxes at least once.
Test case 222: It can be shown that regardless of what we do, we can never use the first box.
Test case 333: We only have 111 pile and can use that directly.
